---
title: "Gendreau Lab Research Hub"
subtitle: "Neurosurgical outcomes research, predictive modeling, and web-based calculators"
---

::: {.hero-banner}
## Translating registry-scale data into usable neurosurgical research tools

The Gendreau Lab develops neurosurgical outcomes research, clinical prediction models, and web-based calculators using national registry and institutional data sources, including NCDB, ACS TQIP, SEER, TriNetX, and local clinical datasets.

[View calculators](calculators.qmd){.btn .btn-primary} [View publications](publications.qmd){.btn .btn-secondary}
:::

## Featured tools

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-4}
::: {.tool-card}
### Intracranial Chordoma Survival Calculator

**Dataset:** National Cancer Database  
**Outcome:** Overall survival  
**Status:** Available

[Launch calculator](https://garingriffith-chordoma-survival-calculator.share.connect.posit.cloud){.btn .btn-primary}
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-4}
::: {.tool-card}
### TBI Discharge & Resource Utilization Calculator

**Dataset:** ACS TQIP 2020–2024  
**Outcomes:** Discharge disposition, ICU admission, mechanical ventilation, and prolonged resource utilization  
**Status:** In development

[View details](calculators.qmd#tbi-discharge-and-resource-utilization-calculator){.btn .btn-secondary}
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-4}
::: {.tool-card}
### Glioblastoma Survival Calculator

**Dataset:** National Cancer Database  
**Outcome:** Overall survival  
**Status:** In development

[View details](calculators.qmd#glioblastoma-survival-calculator){.btn .btn-secondary}
:::
:::
:::

## Research focus areas

- Neuro-oncology outcomes and survival prediction
- Traumatic brain injury prognosis and resource utilization
- Registry-based neurosurgical epidemiology
- Clinical informatics and model-assisted abstraction
- Web-based research calculators and decision-support prototypes
- Vascular and perioperative outcomes research

## Disclaimer

The calculators and materials on this site are intended for research and educational use. They are not medical devices and are not a substitute for professional medical judgment, multidisciplinary discussion, or individualized patient counseling.
